data.title: Add SC1-SC5 behavioral assertions to retargeted onboarding tests
data.description:
## Description

Add new behavioral assertions to the retargeted test files that verify epic success criteria SC1-SC5 exist in onboarding/SKILL.md. These assertions ensure the tests are behavioral (verifying content presence), not just structural path changes.

### Assertions to add

**SC1 — CI keys in config template** (in commands-format or CI autodetection tests):
- Verify ci.fast_gate_job, ci.fast_fail_job, ci.test_ceil_job, ci.integration_workflow appear in onboarding SKILL.md
- Already covered by Python test if retargeted; add equivalent bash assertion

**SC2 — Numbered CI workflow selection**:
- Verify onboarding SKILL.md contains numbered-list selection language when multiple workflows detected
- Grep for: numbered list, selection, ci_workflow_confidence, choose/select

**SC3 — Deprecation notice for merge.ci_workflow_name**:
- Already covered by Python test_skill_mentions_merge_ci_workflow_name_deprecation
- Add bash assertion verifying merge.ci_workflow_name + deprecated/migrate appears

**SC4 — version.file_path disambiguation**:
- Verify onboarding SKILL.md contains numbered selection for multiple version files
- Grep for: version_files + select/choose/numbered

**SC5 — Key name corrections (negative assertions)**:
- Verify jira.project_key does NOT appear (should be jira.project)
- Verify design.system followed by non-underscore does NOT appear (should be design.system_name)
- These are regression guards

### Files to modify
- tests/skills/test-project-setup-commands-format.sh — add SC1, SC5 assertions
- tests/skills/test-project-setup-dryrun-conclusion.sh — add SC4 assertion (version disambiguation)
- tests/skills/test-project-setup-onboarding-step.sh — add SC2 assertion (numbered CI selection)
- tests/skills/test_project_setup_ci_autodetection.py — add SC3 assertion (already partially covered), SC5 negative assertion

### Unit test exemption justification
Same as task 1: these files ARE tests. No source code modified. Meta-tests would be change-detectors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash test files include at least 1 assertion per SC (SC1-SC5)
  Verify: grep -c "SC[1-5]" tests/skills/test-project-setup-*.sh | awk -F: '{sum+=$2} END {exit (sum < 5)}'
- [ ] Negative assertions: jira.project_key must NOT appear in onboarding SKILL.md
  Verify: ! grep -q "jira\.project_key" plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] Negative assertions: design.system (without _name suffix) must NOT appear as a standalone key
  Verify: ! grep -qP "design\.system[^_]" plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md
- [ ] All 4 bash test files pass
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-project-setup-commands-format.sh && bash tests/skills/test-project-setup-dependencies.sh && bash tests/skills/test-project-setup-dryrun-conclusion.sh && bash tests/skills/test-project-setup-onboarding-step.sh
- [ ] Python test passes
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && poetry run pytest tests/skills/test_project_setup_ci_autodetection.py -q
- [ ] `ruff check tests/skills/test_project_setup_ci_autodetection.py` passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check tests/skills/test_project_setup_ci_autodetection.py
